description: R88 fresh09 single-node recheck. Outcome: A.fresh09_timeout_steady_state; fresh09 reproduces R85 5/5 same_failure(timeout), recovery_consecutive_rounds=0. Original cohort C closure NOT claimed; fresh09 NOT recovered; not a closure attempt by design (recovery chain was reset at R85).

summary.accounting_note: divergence_run_count counts runs (a run is a divergence run iff it carries any of the four phase labels app_pre_post_diverged, app_minimal_diverged, minimal_transport_diverged, bridge_io_diverged); divergence_phase_label_count counts phase-label occurrences across those divergence runs (a single run can carry multiple phase labels). label_counts above is per-occurrence, matching divergence_phase_label_breakdown.

interpretation: R88 single-node recheck: fresh09 only. fresh01, fresh15, fresh10, fresh04 and all other fresh nodes were not run.
All 5 runs completed with matrix_status=0 and uniform same_failure labels (probe_io_all_timeout + reality_all_timeout); no divergence phase labels appeared.
fresh09 5/5 same_failure(timeout) reproduces the R85 result; the R85 timeout is steady-state, not transient noise.
fresh09 recovery_consecutive_rounds remains 0 after R88 (chain was reset at R85; no all_ok run in this round).
R88 is not a closure attempt by design: a broken closure chain cannot be patched, restart needs a fresh consecutive sequence beginning with at least one all_ok round.
Original cohort C closure (fresh01+fresh09+fresh15) is NOT claimed: fresh09 remains the broken member; fresh10 closure at R87 was a rotated-replacement closure, not a substitute for fresh09.

fresh09_recheck_status.closure_attempt_blocked_reason: fresh09 recovery chain was reset to 0 at R85 (3/3 same_failure timeout). A broken closure chain cannot be patched; restart requires a fresh consecutive sequence. R88 is therefore a recheck, not a closure attempt.
fresh09_recheck_status.original_cohort_c_closure_reason: Original cohort C identity is fresh01+fresh09+fresh15. fresh09 reproduced 5/5 same_failure(timeout) at R88, confirming the R85 timeout as steady-state. fresh09 is therefore NOT recovered and original cohort C closure remains NOT achieved. fresh10's closure is a rotated-replacement closure for the active set, not a substitute for fresh09's original-cohort identity.

classification.label: fresh09 R85 timeout reproduced as steady-state (single-node recheck, no closure)
classification.rationale: R88 ran the authorized single-node recheck for fresh09 only, 5 runs at example.com:80. All 5 runs completed with matrix_status=0 and uniform same_failure labels (probe_io_all_timeout + reality_all_timeout, classes={timeout:9} per run). No phase labels and no NEW structural divergence. fresh09 reproduces the R85 same_failure(timeout) at 5/5, so the R85 result is steady-state rather than transient noise. R88 is NOT a closure attempt: fresh09's recovery chain was reset to 0 at R85, and a broken chain cannot be patched. recovery_consecutive_rounds remains 0; per-rep recovery closure is NOT achieved.
classification.original_cohort_c_closure_reason: fresh09 reproduces R85 same_failure(timeout) 5/5 at R88; remains broken; fresh10 R87 closure is a rotated-replacement closure, not a substitute for fresh09's original-cohort identity.
classification.next_handling: Do not auto-extend R88. Do not retry failed runs to patch the result. Do not write fresh09 recovered. Do not write whole / original cohort C closure. fresh09 is now confirmed as steady-state same_failure(timeout) at R85 + R88. Next natural authorized round, if desired, would be either: (a) accept fresh09 as steady-state broken and continue the rotated active set (fresh01/fresh15/fresh10) coverage, possibly drawing from R73 round-1-only recovery pool (fresh08/fresh11/fresh12/fresh13/fresh14) for additional rotated reps; or (b) attempt another fresh09 recheck only if there is a hypothesis about why R85+R88 timeouts could be transient (e.g. upstream node maintenance window). Either path requires a new explicit user authorization.

redaction_notes: Evidence uses only the neutral outbound key fresh09.
Raw tag/server/uuid/public_key/short_id/path/header/server_name/password material remains only in local /tmp inputs and is not committed.
subset_schema_gate.violations is committed only because it is the empty array; structural redaction is preserved.
